channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Datachannel Unavailable
item.description: Jul 20, 19:13 UTC Resolved - This incident is now resolved. Jul 20, 19:07 UTC Monitoring - We have identified an issue with Realtime datachannel creation failure and have applied a fix. We are currently monitoring the results.

item.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the country’s leading AI companies. David...

item.title: Disruption with GPT 5.3 Codex
item.description: Jul 20, 17:21 UTC Update - The upstream provider continues to return intermittent errors for GPT 5.3 Codex requests. Some users may experience failed or interrupted responses when using this model. Traffic continues to be automatically rerouted to reduce impact, and we are actively working with the...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 20, 16:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 20, 16:07 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 20, 15:09 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 20, 14:41 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Introducing Cosmos 3 Edge
Kimi K3: The open-weights escalation
item.description: The global implications on the AI ecosystem.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
item.description: A single request should not be able to do this much. But this week, small inputs led to code execution, memory loss, stolen keys, and disabled security tools. The paths were often simple: exposed systems, weak checks, old drivers, fake prompts, and public code used for malware delivery. Some bugs...

item.title: Elevated errors on Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 20, 13:20 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: OpenSSL Silently Fixes ‘HollowByte’ DoS Vulnerability
item.description: Attackers could send waves of malicious payloads to trigger buffer pre-allocations that are not freed, exhausting server memory. The post OpenSSL Silently Fixes ‘HollowByte’ DoS Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But...

item.title: Capital One Open Sources AI-Powered ‘VulnHunter’ Security Tool
item.description: The agentic security tool identifies potentially exploitable code flaws, traces attack paths, and recommends targeted remediations. The post Capital One Open Sources AI-Powered ‘VulnHunter’ Security Tool appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated error rates for Opus 4.5
item.description: Jul 20, 10:05 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jul 20, 07:57 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 20, 07:03 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Hugging Face Hacked in Autonomous AI Attack
item.description: Targeting production infrastructure, the attack compromised internal datasets and service credentials. The post Hugging Face Hacked in Autonomous AI Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and Trend Micro's Zero Day Initiative (ZDI) detailed it on July 15. A fix shipped on June 25 in 7-Zip 26.02....

item.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description: A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet. The findings come from an analysis of 200 Gemini CLI session logs between March 19 and April 21, 2026,...

item.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
item.description: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also develop their own...

item.title: Chrome 150 Update Patches Severe Memory Safety Bugs
item.description: The fresh security update resolves six critical and high-severity use-after-free vulnerabilities. The post Chrome 150 Update Patches Severe Memory Safety Bugs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Kimi K3 Investment Research Capability Benchmarked: Moonshot AI Top Model Now Powers AlphaEngine With First-Tier IRB Scores
item.description: AlphaEngine tests Kimi K3 on proprietary IRB investment research benchmark: K3 beats GPT-5.5 and OPUS-4.8 in temporal judgment, evidence boundary control, and premise correction, already deployed on platform.

item.title: Kimi K3 48-Hour Chip Design Experiment: Why Moonshot AI Autonomous EDA Pipeline Has the Entire Semiconductor Industry Nervous
item.description: Moonshot AI Kimi K3 completes full chip design flow from architecture to layout in 48 hours using open-source EDA tools and 45nm library, challenging EDA incumbents and democratizing chip design.

item.title: ByteDance Releases Seed Audio 1.0: An Audio Generation Model With Fine-Grained Temporal Control for Cinema-Grade Sound Creation
item.description: ByteDance Seed team launches Seed Audio 1.0 audio creation model supporting unified orchestration of speech, sound effects, and ambience with precise timeline control and 20+ language support.

item.title: ZTE Fully Activates Its AI Flywheel: From OEX Supernode and AIDC to Nubia Agent Phone and Humanoid Robots
item.description: ZTE presents end-to-end AI portfolio at WAIC 2026: OEX supernode wins SAIL Star again, Nubia NaviX Ultra ships as global first agent smartphone, humanoid robot family and AIDC solutions complete the picture.

item.title: WAIC 2026 Chip Consensus: Supernodes Are the Future as Every Major GPU Maker Puts Architecture Innovation First
item.description: Every major Chinese GPU maker agrees at WAIC 2026: the era of single-chip competition is over. Supernode architecture, optical interconnection, and system-level TCO define the next computing frontier.

item.title: Fable 5 requiring usage credits on Max plans
item.description: Jul 20, 07:35 UTC Monitoring - Some Claude Code Max plan users were incorrectly prompted to use usage credits to access Fable 5, which is included in their plan. If you were affected, please restart Claude Code.

item.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
item.description: In an ironic twist, open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform Hugging Face revealed that it was the victim of a hack perpetrated by an autonomous AI agent system. The company said it detected and responded to the incident targeting its production infrastructure earlier last week. "We...

item.title: WP2Shell WordPress Vulnerabilities Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Exploitation of the new WordPress vulnerabilities tracked as CVE-2026-60137 and CVE-2026-63030 started soon after disclosure. The post WP2Shell WordPress Vulnerabilities Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: SleeperGem Uses Three Malicious RubyGems Packages to Target Developer Machines
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new software supply chain attack codenamed SleeperGem targeting the Ruby ecosystem after three malicious gems were published to RubyGems with the end goal of serving additional payloads. The rogue gems are listed below - git_credential_manager (versions...

item.title: GraphDx: A Cost-Aware Knowledge-Enhanced Multi-Agent Framework for Sequential Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2607.15280v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sequential diagnosis requires balancing diagnostic accuracy against resource costs through iterative information gathering. Existing Large Language Model (LLM) approaches exhibit a critical knowledge-reasoning gap: despite encoding extensive medical...

item.title: Causal-Audit: Explicit and Auditable Graph-based Reasoning via Target-Aware Causal Chain Construction
item.description: arXiv:2607.15281v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal and intervention-based question answering is fundamental to advancing large language models (LLMs) toward reasoning beyond surface-level correlations and understanding underlying causal mechanisms. However, existing LLM-based methods often rely...

item.title: Cura 1T: Specialized Model for Agentic Healthcare
item.description: arXiv:2607.15314v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Healthcare spans high-stakes communication, expert reasoning, and workflow execution, yet specialized LLMs that cover these use cases together remain limited. A healthcare model must handle patient consultation, clinical reasoning over text and...

item.title: AnovaX: A Local, Multi-Agent Voice Assistant with LLM Planning, Typed Executors, and Adaptive Recovery
item.description: arXiv:2607.15367v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Desktop voice assistants are still dominated by cloud pipelines that ship raw audio off the machine and expose a fixed set of skills. We describe AnovaX, a small local-first assistant that runs entirely on the user's computer and treats the desktop...

item.title: Precise but Uncoupled: Reviewer Precision Does Not Guarantee Critique Uptake in Multi-Agent Math Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2607.15388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many math- and science-oriented agent systems use hierarchical designs with specialized reviewer roles, assuming that a dedicated review stage should help turn wrong candidates into correct ones. We test this assumption on 4,181 verifier-grounded...

item.title: Structure of the Circular-Dyadic Convolution Error
item.description: arXiv:2607.15293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dyadic and circular convolution can both be computed in $O(N\log N)$ time using the Hadamard transform and the FFT-computed discrete Fourier transform (DFT), respectively. The Hadamard transform is preferable for its real-valued sign flips, yet its...

item.title: Position: Quantum Program Generation Must Prioritize Validity Over Probabilistic Scaling
item.description: arXiv:2607.15313v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The scaling hypothesis assumes that increasing model parameters yields emergent reasoning capabilities. This position paper argues that applying this probabilistic paradigm to generic quantum circuit synthesis is a directional error. Unlike natural...

item.title: A Transportable Threshold-Based Framework for Interpretable Classification of Medical Data
item.description: arXiv:2607.15394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Black-box models limit the adoption of artificial intelligence in medicine due to their lack of interpretability and reproducibility. We introduce a statistically grounded framework that provides fully interpretable, rule-based clinical classification...

item.title: Regularity-Aware Stochastic MGDA with Adaptive Conflict-Avoidant Update Direction Control
item.description: arXiv:2607.15412v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-objective learning (MOL) aims to optimize multiple objectives simultaneously. The multi-gradient descent algorithm (MGDA) is a workhorse that iteratively updates along a common descent or conflict-avoidant (CA) direction across objectives. In...

item.title: AI Trading: Evaluating Large Language Models for Technical Market Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2607.15414v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as powerful tools for processing the heterogeneous information environments of modern financial markets. This paper presents a systematic, comparative evaluation of five prominent LLMs: GPT-4 Turbo, Claude 3...

item.title: Large Language Models as Unified Multimodal Learners for Clinical Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.15380v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electronic health records combine free-text clinical narratives with structured measurements such as vital signs, laboratory values, and comorbidities. Yet most clinical prediction systems still rely on task-specific fusion architectures, pairing...

item.title: Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.15495v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Out of everything the human brain processes, only a small fraction is consciously accessible, in the sense of being available for verbal report, deliberate control, and flexible reasoning. In this paper, we present evidence that an analogous...

item.title: VarRate: Training-Free Variable-Rate KV Cache Compression for Long-Context LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.15498v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The key-value (KV) cache is the main memory bottleneck in long-context large language model (LLM) inference. Two leading training-free families are both structurally limited: token-selection methods (SnapKV, Ada-KV) score importance from an...

item.title: EpiNarrate: Agentic Generation of Grounded Narratives from Epidemiological Scenario Projections
item.description: arXiv:2607.15544v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generation of clear and accessible public health narratives is critical for communicating complex epidemiological projections to policymakers and the general public at large. Such narratives require more than simply reporting numbers: projections must...

item.title: SkillCorpus: Consolidating and Evaluating the Open Skill Ecosystem for Real-World LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.15557v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent skills, SKILL.md files that package reusable procedural knowledge for an LLM agent, are a popular mechanism for extending agent capabilities. Public repositories now host them in large and growing numbers, yet these artifacts are fragmented,...

item.title: Incident with GitHub Actions
item.description: Jul 20, 03:03 UTC Update - API Requests is operating normally. Jul 20, 02:43 UTC Update - Issues, API Requests, and Pages have recovered. We are continuing to work on restoring GitHub Actions jobs using self-hosted or larger-hosted runners. Jul 20, 02:23 UTC Update - API Requests is experiencing...

item.title: Elevated errors for GitHub-dependent ChatGPT and Codex workflows
item.description: Status: InvestigatingUsers may experience failures or delays in ChatGPT and Codex workflows that depend on GitHub. This mostly should impact Codex web and code review for PRs on Github. This is related to an active GitHub API partial outage and degraded GitHub Actions....

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 20, 01:46 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 20, 01:45 UTC Update - Git LFS API success rates have returned to normal. We will...

item.title: Elevated errors affecting ChatGPT
item.description: Status: MonitoringService has been restored and we are continuing to monitor.Affected components Voice mode (Operational) Conversations (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) ChatGPT Work (Operational)

item.title: Workers and Pages builds stuck intializing.
item.description: Jul 18, 21:00 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare is aware of an issue initializing Workers & Pages Builds from 22:05 - 22:45 UTC. The problem has now been resolved.

item.title: Workers and Pages builds unable to start
item.description: Jul 18, 16:46 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue impacting Workers & Pages Builds from starting. We are working to analyze and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Some users are unable to access Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components CLI (Degraded performance) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Degraded performance)

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day

item.title: Codex 5.6-sol Experiencing Increased Server-Overload Errors
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.
